user_prompt: read `lib\ClockAgent\readme.md` and implement inside `lib\ClockAgent`, then update any existing usages of the RTC to use the ClockAgent
agent_reply: - Provides a global `Clock` object with an RTC-like API:
- Reads cache the RTC timestamp and advance it using `millis()`, avoiding repeated I2C RTC reads.
- Writes still call through to `M5.Rtc` and update the cached clock immediately.
Updated existing RTC reads to use `Clock` in:
`M5.Rtc` is now only used inside `ClockAgent`, where it belongs.

user_prompt: first, look at `inc\version.h`, implement the strings in `src\version.c` using as much of compiler preprocessor definitions as possible.
`syncToCompileTime` needs to use `compiler_time_str`, if `compiler_time_str` is in the future, sync the physical RTC to it.
somehow make sure `version.c` is compiled every build, maybe something to do with `platformio.ini`, maybe adding a script to `tools`
it is acceptable if `src\version.c` is automatically generated by a tool, as long as `inc\version.h` stays consistent
do not touch other files not mentioned
agent_reply: - Added [tools/generate_version_source.py](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/tools/generate_version_source.py), which regenerates `src/version.c` before every build so `__DATE__` / `__TIME__` are fresh.
- Updated [platformio.ini](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini) to run that generator as a pre-build script.
- If RTC is invalid, or compile time is newer, writes compile time to the physical RTC.
- Otherwise syncs the software clock state from the existing RTC.
Verification: `pio run` passed. I also ran a second build and confirmed PlatformIO recompiled `src/version.c.o` again, so the pre-build regeneration is doing its job.

- Updated [platformio.ini](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/platformio.ini) to run that generator as a pre-build script.

- Otherwise syncs the software clock state from the existing RTC.

user_prompt: `void CloudUpload::taskMain()` needs to be have the internals of the `for (PendingFile* item = head; item; item = item->next)` loop be broken out in a way that allows for easy calling/testing for just one file
so whatever function you've broken that out into needs another layer of caller that specifies a file, builds a PendingFile structure, and frees the structure when done
also `WiFiClientSecure` needs to be actually secure as much as possible, the destination needs to be authentic
`ClockAgent` must make sure `WiFiClientSecure` works as intended
agent_reply: - Added `CloudUpload::uploadSingleFile(destination, path, timeout_ms)` for easy one-file testing.
- Broke the per-file upload work out into `uploadPendingFile(...)`, shared by both the normal scan loop and the single-file wrapper.
- The single-file path builds a `PendingFile`, calls the shared uploader, then frees it.
- HTTPS is now required for cloud uploads; `http://` is rejected.
- `WiFiClientSecure` now uses the ESP x509 cert bundle via `setCACertBundle(...)`, with hostname verification handled by the secure client connect path.
- `ClockAgent` now pushes its RTC/managed time into system time and exposes `ensureSystemTimeForTls()`, which CloudUpload calls before TLS handshakes.
I did not run PlatformIO, since you said it was busy. Static checks only.
